goal: While Chris was away from his PC and could not playtest live: confirm his 'no camp skeletons on Continue' observation was expected behavior (not a bug), then build only work that does not require live verification - camp enemy variety, menu polish, a batch of playtest-reported bugs/tuning, a web-build size audit, P1/P2 sprite prep (staged only, not wired), and a coverage-check addition - stopping cleanly once genuinely unattended-safe work ran out.
completed: Verified 'no camp skeletons on Continue' against the code: expected (a save past the camp clears it as already-done); a New Game keeps them. No code change needed for this.
Slice B: added a stationary BombEnemy (wind-up + AoE detonate + gas VFX, defusable with one hit) to the forest camp; camp is now 3 skeletons + 1 bombschroom
Added mouse (hover-highlight + click-activate) as a second input path on the title and pause menus, keyboard untouched
Dressed the title screen: dimmed baked-world backdrop slice, UI_Frames NinePatch panel, pixel font, looping campfire critter - all reused existing assets/idioms
Bumped SAVE_VERSION 1->2 so pre-fix saves auto-invalidate to a clean New Game

Fixed the boss 'Icene' font-smear bug at its root: authored assets/game/CuteFantasy-5x9.ttf.import with subpixel_positioning=0, fixing every centered pixel-font Label project-wide (boss name, title, pause menu, HUD) in one change
Shortened the dodge-roll distance (ROLL_SPEED 150->105) and added two more staged Irene boss taunts (3/4 HP and 1/4 HP, alongside the existing half-HP line)

Ran a full collision-correctness audit of prop_table.gd beyond the originally-reported rocks: found and fixed missing colliders (berry bush, camp keg) and severely undersized building colliders (the inn's collider only covered 90px of a ~230px-wide building - most of the facade was walk-through; also cottages and the barn)

Removed the procedural ore-scatter render pass (the actual source of both the walk-through rocks AND the duplicated near-house rock clusters - one root cause, two symptoms) and re-baked the ground image
Fixed a doubled-duck bug (the '$' symbol was both a static prop-table duck AND the separately-spawned animated Ariana node at the same cell) and one flower-overlapping-goose placement bug

Built an AmbientAnimal animator giving land animals idle-bob + slow wander (water animals stay static); fixed Ariana's own sprite framing bug in the process (was displaying as a 2x2 block of 4 ducks instead of 1)

Investigated the modular 64x64 player character sheet and confirmed idle/walk/attack/roll rows can now be mapped with high confidence, and that P1/P2 hair-color distinction needs zero recoloring (the pack ships pre-made hair-color overlay sheets) - documented as a plan in docs/player-modular-mapping.md, explicitly NOT wired into gameplay per the standing rule

Audited the web export bundle: confirmed gdignored seasonal packs (Halloween/Desert/etc) are correctly excluded already; found and gdignored three large fully-unreferenced base-pack directories (Player/, Tiles/, Icons/), cutting the imported asset payload from 4.69 MB to 2.03 MB (57% reduction)
Staged (copied, did not wire) the P1/P2 base body + black/brown hair overlay sprites into assets/game/ as prep for a future approved slice

Wrote tools/check_assets.py: a new automated safety check (in the same fail-loud style as check_symbols.py) verifying every referenced asset exists and isn't accidentally gdignored, plus consistency between the new ANIMAL_ANIM table and the prop table
Wrote docs/playtest-checklist.md consolidating every item awaiting Chris's live confirmation into one ordered top-to-bottom pass, each with its tuning knob
Wrote a wrap artifact to the outbox and pushed after the previous work batch (visible in git log as 'dos outbox: session artifact')
discovered: The three surface-level playtest bugs (spawn near camp, unfair respawn destination, camp enemies reappearing) traced back to only two root causes: player_select never called Game.reset_run() (so every launch silently auto-resumed the last save), and _reset_area respawned ALL of an area's enemies whenever the area wasn't FULLY cleared (so a partial-clear death repopulated already-killed enemies) - these were fixed in an earlier part of this session, confirmed working before tonight's batch began
player.respawn_point was written in three places but read nowhere - dead code that looked functional but wasn't

Godot's .gdignore is evaluated per-directory, not inherited from a parent pack folder - the base paid Cute Fantasy pack itself has no .gdignore even though sibling seasonal packs do, so several assets (Bombschroom, Campfire_Anim) could be referenced with zero copying, while three of its OWN large subdirectories (Player/, Tiles/, Icons/) were being bundled unused purely because nobody had gdignored them specifically

The walk-through rocks and the duplicated near-house rock clusters (two separately-reported bugs) were actually the same procedural ore-scatter render pass - it had no collision node (walk-through) and was blind to building placement (duplication near houses)

Ariana's animated duck sprite was silently broken since it was first wired: her Duck.png sheet has 32px frames but was configured with hframes=4 (64px), so each 'frame' shown was actually a 2x2 block of 4 ducks

The pack's font (CuteFantasy-5x9.ttf) had no committed .import file, so Godot was using default subpixel-positioning=Auto, which is what caused the boss name to render as 'Icene' - a single explicit .import fixes it for every centered pixel-font Label in the project at once

A prop's collider being present is not the same as it being correctly SIZED - the collision audit found colliders that existed but only covered a fraction of the visible sprite footprint (the inn building being the worst case, ~40% coverage), a class of bug that a simple presence/absence check would have missed
decisions: Camp respawn/checkpoint rework treats checkpoint recording as progress-based (furthest area walked past) rather than trigger-volume-based, and treats area reset as 'rearm survivors, leave the dead dead' rather than 'respawn everything not fully cleared' - both decisions stand as the new system behavior
Camp enemy mix is fixed at 3 skeletons + 1 bombschroom per Chris's explicit choice

P1/P2 distinct sprites will use hair-overlay compositing (same body + a different pre-made hair-color sheet) rather than any recoloring approach - recoloring was the original blocker and this sidesteps it entirely

Web-build trimming uses .gdignore (the same mechanism already proven for the seasonal packs) rather than export_filter/exclude_filter in export_presets.cfg, since gdignore is the pattern already validated in this codebase

P1/P2 sprite assets are staged (copied into assets/game/) but deliberately not wired into player.gd/player.tscn/any gameplay logic - wiring requires Chris present to verify each animation live, per his standing rule reiterated this session
lessons: When multiple 'obviously wrong' bugs are reported together, diagnose them as one system before patching symptoms individually - three surface reports (spawn location, respawn destination, enemies reappearing) traced to two root causes, and two more surface reports (walk-through rocks, duplicated rocks near houses) traced to one root cause (procedural ore scatter)
A collision audit needs to check collider SIZE correctness, not just presence - 'does this have a collider' passed for the inn, but the collider only covered the front door, not the walls

Before excluding a directory from the build via .gdignore, grep every scene/script/resource for res:// references into it AND confirm there are no dynamically-constructed load paths (string concatenation) that a static grep would miss - both were verified this session before trimming

Sprite framing bugs (hframes/vframes mismatched to a sheet's actual per-frame pixel size) can silently ship and look 'mostly fine' at a glance (Ariana still looked duck-shaped, just showed 4 ducks per frame) - worth spot-checking sheet dimensions against configured frame counts, not just visually eyeballing the result

A font's centered-text smearing on a pixel font is very likely a subpixel-positioning import setting, not a size/layout bug - worth checking the .import file (or lack thereof) early rather than adjusting Label offsets/anchors
open_questions: Does the bombschroom's balance (22px detonate trigger, 30px blast, 0.6s windup, 3hp) feel fair once Chris plays it, or does it need tuning?
Does the shortened dash (ROLL_SPEED 105) feel right, or was it over/under-corrected?
Do the widened building colliders (especially the inn at 210px) feel correctly sized in practice, or too generous/tight anywhere?
Does the animal wander radius/speed read as natural ambient life, or too twitchy/too subtle?
Is Chris comfortable approving the P1/P2 modular sprite mapping plan (docs/player-modular-mapping.md) so that slice can be scheduled with him present to verify animations live?
intent: Ended the session after explicitly running out of work that could be done safely without Chris watching live - the remaining tasks (wiring P1/P2 sprites into gameplay, Boss Phase 2, any further feel/tuning) all require his live judgment per his own instruction not to guess at them.
next_task: Chris runs docs/playtest-checklist.md top-to-bottom on Windows to verify tonight's full batch (title screen, mouse menus, collision fixes, bombschroom, animal wander, font fix, Irene taunts, pause menu) in one ordered pass; then either approves the P1/P2 sprite mapping plan to start that slice with him present, or flags anything from the checklist that needs adjustment.
dont_retry: Don't assume a pack sub-directory is gdignored just because sibling/seasonal packs are - check each specific folder individually; the base pack itself had three large unreferenced subdirectories nobody had gdignored
Don't treat 'has a collider' as sufficient collision-correctness verification - also check the collider's SIZE against the sprite's actual visible footprint

Don't wire spritesheet hframes/vframes purely from nominal pixel dimensions without decoding and visually confirming the actual per-frame grid - Ariana's duck sheet shipped with a silent 4x framing bug this way
